Libdl: In `Libdl.dlpath`, throw an ArgumentError if the handle is `C_NULL` (#61307)

🤖 Discovered by Codex during an audit.

Other functions in Libdl (such as `Libdl.dlsym`) already include a check
for `C_NULL`. For example:


https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/bc4bb7ae9a49808a929917d921e597906a9f30cb/base/libdl.jl#L59-L60

So this PR adds a `C_NULL` check to `Libdl.dlpath`, and (similar to
`Libdl.dlsym`), throws an `ArgumentError`.

🤖 Co-authored-by: Codex.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Add upgrade mode and daily update check

* Add upgrade mode and daily update check

Implement a Codex-native upgrade audit mode, add a daily scheduled GitHub Actions check, and document the new maintenance workflow.

Validation:
- bun run typecheck
- bun run smoke

Closes: #5

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* Add upgrade-check spec coverage

Add a dedicated upgrade-check spec and wire it into smoke so workflow changes land with real test coverage and the review gate stays meaningful.

Validation:
- bun run typecheck
- bun run smoke

Refs: #5

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Refs: #3

repository.name: habits
repository.description: A tracking app for my personal healthy daily habits, initially focused on workout tracking with rotating suggestions.

commit.message:
drop redundant reduce-block fallback

Use blocks[split_index..] directly since the stage classifier guarantees a valid split index and Ruby returns an empty array at the array end.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: jrf
repository.description: JSON transformer with the power and speed of Ruby

commit.message:
Fix invalid date when saving today's weight

Wire the Today card weight buttons through a real function call so the click event is no longer passed into openWeightModal as the date value. Also add a defensive date-format guard inside openWeightModal so invalid callers fall back to today instead of saving a bad date to Supabase.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: habits

commit.message:
deduplicate frame-reader compaction callsites

Keep the simple compaction policy, but use a tiny local compact! helper so the two early-return paths in next_payload do not repeat the same buffer-trimming code.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: jrf
repository.description: JSON transformer with the power and speed of Ruby

commit.message:
rename frame partial-state predicate

Use has_partial? instead of finished_cleanly? so the reader exposes its concrete state and the worker-pool error check reads directly as a transport-policy decision.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: jrf
repository.description: JSON transformer with the power and speed of Ruby

commit.message:
move frame EOF policy to worker pool

Replace ParallelFrameReader#finish! with a simple finished_cleanly? predicate so the reader reports state and the worker-pool caller decides that trailing bytes at EOF are a transport error.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: jrf
repository.description: JSON transformer with the power and speed of Ruby

commit.message:
inline frame-reader compaction helpers

Keep finish! as the explicit EOF validation step, but inline the tiny compaction helpers inside next_payload and finish! now that the simpler compaction rule no longer needs extra indirection.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: jrf
repository.description: JSON transformer with the power and speed of Ruby

commit.message:
simplify parallel frame compaction

Compact the consumed prefix only when the frame reader cannot yield another full payload or when finishing, instead of carrying an extra threshold heuristic tuned for small reads.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
chore: lintとformatの実行設定を簡潔にする

package.json の不要な check スクリプトを削除し、lint と format をリポジトリ直下指定へ簡略化する。
Taskfile 側で check の合成を担う形に戻し、設定ファイルの ignore と整形差分を最小限反映する。

commit.message:
unify runner parallel entrypoint

Make parallelism an option on Runner#run instead of a separate public execution mode, so the CLI and runner share one execution contract and one fallback decision point.

Also tighten the -P help text to describe its opportunistic map-prefix behavior.

commit.message:
unify runner input ownership

Make the CLI runner own file inputs in both serial and parallel paths instead of threading file_paths separately through parallel-only APIs.

This removes a misleading seam from the PR and keeps the runner interface aligned with its internal state.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Reduce pull request summary complexity

Co-Authored-By: Codex by OpenAI <codex@openai.com>

simplify runner value flow

Factor serial and parallel execution around the same flow: build a value stream, optionally apply reducer stages, then emit once.

Replace the old mixed pipeline/emission helpers with small stream-oriented helpers so the PR reads as an integrated design change instead of an ad-hoc patch.

test(simulator): add split-domain EL3202 recovery scenario

Summary:
- add scenario 37 for the captured EL3202 reconnect PREOP timeout with
  digital loopback on a separate PDO domain
- update the simulator README catalog and next-directions text to mark
  the split-domain captured-device follow-up as covered

Rationale:
- continue the numbered simulator loop from test/integration/simulator/README.md
  with the next concrete captured-device regression after scenario 36
- pin the domain split explicitly so the suite proves RTD recovery can
  degrade its own domain without falsely degrading unrelated loopback I/O

Validation:
- mix test test/integration/simulator/37_split_domain_el3202_reconnect_preop_timeout_test.exs
- mix test test/integration/simulator
- Found by:
  test/integration/simulator/37_split_domain_el3202_reconnect_preop_timeout_test.exs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

test(simulator): fold transport baseline into scenario loop

Summary:
- add 00_healthy_ring_transport_matrix.{md,test.exs} for the
  transport-aware healthy ring baseline across UDP, raw, and
  redundant raw
- remove the ad hoc ring_test.exs and
  redundant_raw_ring_test.exs coverage and point the README at the
  numbered scenario path

Rationale:
- keep simulator transport coverage aligned with the cyclic scenario
  workflow documented in test/integration/simulator/README.md
- make UDP assertions explicit so the baseline still exercises UDP
  even when ETHERCAT_INTEGRATION_TRANSPORT defaults helper scenarios
  to raw

Validation:
- mix test test/integration/simulator/00_healthy_ring_transport_matrix_test.exs
- mix test test/integration/simulator
- Scenario:
  test/integration/simulator/00_healthy_ring_transport_matrix_test.exs
